content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string SSS consisting of lowercase English characters. Chef defined functions left shift L(X)L(X)L(X) and right shift R(X)R(X)R(X) as follows.


L(X)L(X)L(X) is defined as shifting all characters of string XXX  one step towards left and moving the first character to the end.
R(X)R(X)R(X) is defined as shifting all characters of string XXX one step towards the right and moving the last character to the beginning.
For example, L("abcd") = "bcda" and R("abcd") = "dabc"

Chef wants to find out whether there exists a string VVV of the same length as SSS such that both L(V)=SL(V) = SL(V)=S and R(V)=SR(V) = SR(V)=S holds.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a string SSS.
###Output:
For each test case, If there exists a valid string VVV, print "YES", otherwise print "NO" (without the quotes).

###Constraints


1≤T≤1001 \le T \le 1001≤T≤100
1≤∣S∣≤1061 \le |S| \le 10^61≤∣S∣≤106
SSS contains all lower case English alphabets.
It's guaranteed that the total length of the strings in one test file doesn't exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, Chef can choose V=SV = SV=S
In the second test case, Chef can choose VVV = "ba" which satisfies both conditions.
There doesn't exist any valid choice for string VVV in the third test case.
